source: Learning From Positive and Unlabeled Data- A Survey.pdf

concepts.finding: PU learning addresses training with only positive labels and unlabeled data (which may contain both positives and negatives). Key approaches: treating unlabeled as negative with class prior correction, biased SVM, risk-estimation-based methods.
concepts.implication: PU learning is directly applicable to search where we observe clicks (positive) but lack explicit negative feedback; unclicked documents may be irrelevant or unseen.
concepts.open_question: How should the class prior (fraction of positives in unlabeled data) be estimated for click-based search logs?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A PU learning approach with estimated class prior will improve search ranking NDCG by 5%+ over treating unclicked items as negative examples.
